description: did:key signing/verification golden vector (RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.4 / §5.11.1, acdp/0.2.0). Real Ed25519 keypair; the agent_id IS the key: did:key:z<base58btc(0xed01 + raw_public_key)>, and signature.key_id carries the same identifier as its fragment. Resolution is pure — no network, no DID document, no assertionMethod check. The body carries acdp_version '0.2.0' explicitly, as 0.2.0 producers MUST (RFC-ACDP-0001 §6). Test keypair MUST NOT be used in production. (The plan name 'sig-002-did-key-golden' was already taken by the ECDSA-P256 golden; this is the did:key golden.)

test_keypair.warning: TEST ONLY. The private seed is publicly known. Never use this keypair for real publications.

vectors.name: Minimal first-version 0.2.0 publish request, did:key producer, signed

producer_content.title: Golden test vector — did:key first version

publish_request_body.title: Golden test vector — did:key first version

registry_assigned.note: Identifier assignment is method-independent; any registry advertising did:key mints exactly as for did:web.

verification_steps: 1. Derive the did:key identity: 'did:key:z' + base58btc(0xed01 || public_key_raw_32_bytes); MUST equal test_keypair.did_key.

2. did:key resolution is PURE (RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.11.1): decode the multibase 'z' payload, require multicodec prefix 0xed01, extract the 32 key bytes — no network, no DID document, no assertionMethod check.
3. The key_id fragment MUST byte-equal the DID's method-specific identifier (W3C did:key convention).
4. JCS-canonicalize producer_content; MUST equal expected.canonical_form (note acdp_version is inside the signed bytes).
5. SHA-256 → expected.content_hash; sign the ASCII bytes of the full content_hash string; MUST equal expected.signature_value_base64; verify with the public key.
